content.text: Now let me examine the task file to understand the complete context:

content.content:
1→---
     2→task: h-implement-complete-cli-integration
     3→branch: feature/complete-cli-integration
     4→status: in-progress
     5→created: 2025-09-05
     6→started: 2025-09-05
     7→modules: [internal/cli/transflow, cmd/ploy, internal/orchestration]
     8→---
     9→
    10→# Complete CLI Integration for Transflow MVP
    11→
    12→## Problem/Goal
    13→Complete the end-to-end CLI integration for `ploy transflow run` to make it fully functional per the MVP requirements. While the CLI infrastructure exists, the MVP.md shows "Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`)" as "❌ Not Implemented", indicating missing functionality or integration issues that prevent the full transflow workflow from working properly.
    14→
    15→The goal is to ensure `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml` executes the complete workflow: recipe execution → build validation → healing (if needed) → GitLab MR creation, with proper error handling and comprehensive testing.
    16→
    17→## Success Criteria
    18→- [ ] Full end-to-end workflow execution via `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`
    19→- [ ] Integration with existing ARF pipeline for recipe execution
    20→- [ ] Build validation using `SharedPush` to controller `/v1/apps/:app/builds`
    21→- [ ] LangGraph healing integration (planner/reducer jobs) when builds fail
    22→- [ ] GitLab MR creation after successful workflow completion
    23→- [ ] Comprehensive CLI argument parsing and validation
    24→- [ ] Support for all documented CLI flags and options
    25→- [ ] Unit tests for CLI command parsing and workflow orchestration
    26→- [ ] Create `ploy-orw-java11-maven` test repository, push to GitLab, and update tests to use real repo URL instead of fake test/repo URLs
    27→- [ ] Integration tests demonstrating full workflow functionality
    28→- [ ] Error handling that follows project patterns (graceful failures)
    29→- [ ] Verbose and quiet output modes working correctly
    30→- [ ] Working examples using the provided transflow.yaml template
    31→- [ ] Documentation updates showing CLI usage and examples
    32→- [ ] Mark MVP.md task as completed (✅)
    33→- [ ] Update CHANGELOG.md with CLI integration completion
    34→- [ ] All tests passing with TDD methodology (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)
    35→
    36→## Context Files
    37→- @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md - Current MVP status and requirements
    38→- @roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml - Configuration template and examples
    39→- @internal/cli/transflow/run.go - CLI entrypoint and command parsing
    40→- @internal/cli/transflow/runner.go - Core workflow execution engine
    41→- @internal/cli/transflow/config.go - YAML configuration loading and validation
    42→- @cmd/ploy/main.go:47-48 - Main CLI integration point
    43→- @internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush - Build validation interface
    44→- @internal/orchestration - Job submission and monitoring infrastructure
    45→- @internal/git/provider - GitLab MR integration (recently implemented)
    46→
    47→## User Notes
    48→- Follow TDD methodology strictly: RED (failing tests) → GREEN (minimal implementation) → REFACTOR (VPS testing)
    49→- Reuse existing infrastructure where possible (ARF, orchestration, git operations)
    50→- Follow CLI patterns established in other `ploy` commands
    51→- Ensure proper error handling and user feedback for all failure modes
    52→- Test both success and failure scenarios comprehensively
    53→- Validate integration with recently completed GitLab MR functionality
    54→- Use the transflow.yaml template for testing scenarios
    55→
    56→## Context Manifest
    57→
    58→### How the Complete CLI Integration Currently Works: Transflow MVP Architecture
    59→
    60→When a developer runs `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`, the system initiates a complex multi-stage workflow that orchestrates OpenRewrite recipe execution, build validation, self-healing (if enabled), and GitLab merge request creation. This end-to-end integration spans multiple architectural layers and requires careful coordination between existing ARF infrastructure, job orchestration systems, and git provider integrations.
    61→
    62→**Current CLI Entry Point and Parsing:**
    63→The main CLI integration begins in `/cmd/ploy/main.go:47-48` where the `transflow` command routes to `transflow.TransflowCmd(os.Args[2:], controllerURL)` in `/internal/cli/transflow/run.go`. The `TransflowCmd` function handles the primary command parsing, supporting only the `run` subcommand which delegates to `runTransflow()`. This function creates a comprehensive flag set supporting multiple execution modes including dry-run validation (`--dry-run`), specialized job rendering (`--render-planner`, `--plan`, `--reduce`), selective execution (`--exec-llm-first`, `--exec-orw-first`, `--apply-first`), and standard workflow execution.
    64→
    65→**Configuration Loading and Validation:**
    66→The system loads YAML configuration from `/roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` using `LoadConfig()` in `/internal/cli/transflow/config.go`. The configuration structure includes workflow metadata (`id`, `target_repo`, `base_ref`), build parameters (`lane`, `build_timeout`), recipe steps, and optional self-healing configuration. The `SelfHealConfig` struct supports enabling/disabling healing, setting maximum retry attempts (1-5), and configuring cooldown periods. Configuration validation ensures required fields are present and validates duration formats for timeouts and cooldowns.
    67→
    68→**Integration Factory Pattern:**
    69→The CLI creates a `TransflowIntegrations` factory in `/internal/cli/transflow/integrations.go` that provides concrete implementations for all workflow components. The factory method `CreateConfiguredRunner()` instantiates a `TransflowRunner` and injects four critical dependencies: `ARFGitOperations` (wrapping existing ARF git operations), `ARFRecipeExecutor` (delegating to `ploy arf transform` commands), `SharedPushBuildChecker` (using existing `SharedPush` for build validation), and `GitLabProvider` (for merge request creation). This dependency injection pattern enables comprehensive testing with mock implementations.
    70→
    71→**Workflow Orchestration Engine:**
    72→The `TransflowRunner` in `/internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` executes a seven-step workflow: (1) clone repository using ARF git operations, (2) create timestamped workflow branch (`workflow/{id}/{timestamp}`), (3) execute OpenRewrite recipes by invoking `ploy arf transform` for each recipe ID, (4) commit changes with standardized commit message, (5) run build check using `SharedPush` against controller `/v1/apps/:app/builds` endpoint, (6) push branch to remote repository, and (7) create GitLab merge request if configured. Each step is instrumented with timing and success tracking via `StepResult` objects.
    73→
    74→**Build Validation Integration:**
    75→Build checking leverages the existing `SharedPush` function from `/internal/cli/common/deploy.go` configured with `IsPlatform=false` (ploy mode), `Environment=dev` (sandbox), and a generated app name format `tfw-{id}-{timestamp}`. The system uses lane detection if not explicitly specified and respects configurable build timeouts. Build failures trigger the self-healing workflow if enabled, otherwise cause immediate workflow termination.
    76→
    77→**Self-Healing Workflow Architecture:**
    78→When builds fail and self-healing is enabled, the system initiates a sophisticated three-phase healing process: planner job submission, parallel fanout execution, and reducer job evaluation. The planner job analyzes the build error and generates a `plan.json` with multiple healing options (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated). The fanout orchestrator in `/internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestrator.go` executes healing branches in parallel with first-success-wins semantics and configurable parallelism limits. The reducer job evaluates all results and determines next actions (typically "stop" if a branch succeeded).
    79→
    80→**Job Submission Infrastructure:**
    81→The healing workflow uses Nomad job templates from `/roadmap/transflow/jobs/` including `planner.hcl`, `reducer.hcl`, `llm_exec.hcl`, and `orw_apply.hcl`. Job submission occurs through `/internal/orchestration/submit.go` using `SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` for batch jobs that must reach terminal states. Environment variable substitution handles model configuration (`TRANSFLOW_MODEL`), MCP tools (`TRANSFLOW_TOOLS`), execution limits (`TRANSFLOW_LIMITS`), and run identification. The system supports both local file output and SeaweedFS/HTTP artifact retrieval patterns.
    82→
    83→**GitLab Merge Request Integration:**
    84→The recently implemented GitLab integration in `/internal/git/provider/gitlab.go` handles MR creation and updates through GitLab's REST API v4. The system extracts project namespace/name from HTTPS repository URLs, searches for existing MRs with the same source branch for idempotency, and creates descriptive MR content including applied transformations, healing summaries (if applicable), and standardized labels ("ploy", "tfl"). Authentication uses `GITLAB_TOKEN` environment variable with optional `GITLAB_URL` for custom instances.
    85→
    86→**Error Handling and Recovery:**
    87→The system implements graceful error handling at multiple levels. Configuration validation catches malformed YAML early. Git operations include proper cleanup of temporary directories. Build failures optionally trigger healing workflows rather than immediate termination. Job submissions include timeout handling and terminal state monitoring. MR creation failures are logged but don't fail the overall workflow since they're considered optional. The `TransflowResult` structure captures comprehensive step-by-step execution details for debugging.
    88→
    89→### For Complete CLI Integration Implementation: Current Gaps and Requirements
    90→
    91→The MVP roadmap shows "Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`)" as "❌ Not Implemented" despite extensive infrastructure existing. Analysis reveals the integration is substantially complete but has critical gaps preventing full end-to-end functionality.
    92→
    93→**Missing Production Job Submission:**
    94→The most significant gap is in `/internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go` where the `SubmitPlannerJob()` and `SubmitReducerJob()` methods contain placeholder implementations that return "production job submission not implemented yet" errors. The job submission helper needs production implementations that use the existing `orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()` function with properly rendered HCL templates from the asset rendering methods already implemented in the runner.
    95→
    96→**Incomplete Asset Rendering Integration:**
    97→While `RenderPlannerAssets()`, `RenderLLMExecAssets()`, and `RenderORWApplyAssets()` exist in the runner, the job submission helpers don't properly connect these rendered assets to actual job submission. The production implementation needs to: (1) call the appropriate asset rendering method, (2) perform environment variable substitution on the HCL templates, (3) write the rendered HCL to the workspace, and (4) submit the job using `orchestration.SubmitAndWaitTerminal()`.
    98→
    99→**Schema Validation Integration:**
   100→The CLI includes sophisticated validation infrastructure in `/internal/cli/transflow/schema.go` using JSON schema validation for plan.json and next.json artifacts, but these validators (`validatePlanJSON`, `validateNextJSON`) are referenced in the CLI but not implemented. The schema validation ensures planner and reducer outputs conform to expected structures before processing.
   101→
   102→**Fanout Orchestrator Production Mode:**
   103→The `fanoutOrchestrator` in `/internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestrator.go` currently only supports test mode job submission. The production implementation needs to integrate with actual Nomad job submission for parallel healing branch execution.
   104→
   105→**Integration Testing Coverage:**
   106→While unit tests exist for individual components, comprehensive integration tests demonstrating the complete end-to-end workflow are missing. The system needs integration tests that validate: configuration loading → asset rendering → job submission → artifact retrieval → result processing.
   107→
   108→**Configuration Template Validation:**
   109→The transflow.yaml template in `/roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` contains comprehensive configuration options (budgets, network policies, gates) that aren't fully implemented in the current configuration parser. The production system needs to support or gracefully handle all documented configuration options.
   110→
   111→### Technical Reference Details
   112→
   113→#### Core Interface Signatures
   114→
   115→```go
   116→// Primary CLI entry point
   117→func TransflowCmd(args []string, controllerURL string)
   118→func runTransflow(args []string, controllerURL string) error
   119→
   120→// Configuration and validation
   121→func LoadConfig(path string) (*TransflowConfig, error)
   122→func (c *TransflowConfig) Validate() error
   123→func (c *TransflowConfig) ParseBuildTimeout() (time.Duration, error)
   124→
   125→// Integration factory
   126→func NewTransflowIntegrations(controllerURL, workDir string) *TransflowIntegrations
   127→func (i *TransflowIntegrations) CreateConfiguredRunner(config *TransflowConfig) (*TransflowRunner, error)
   128→
   129→// Core workflow execution
   130→func (r *TransflowRunner) Run(ctx context.Context) (*TransflowResult, error)
   131→func (r *TransflowRunner) RenderPlannerAssets() (*PlannerAssets, error)
   132→func (r *TransflowRunner) PrepareRepo(ctx context.Context) (string, string, error)
   133→func (r *TransflowRunner) ApplyDiffAndBuild(ctx context.Context, repoPath, diffPath string) error
   134→
   135→// Job submission interfaces (need production implementation)
   136→type JobSubmissionHelper interface {
   137→    SubmitPlannerJob(ctx context.Context, config *TransflowConfig, buildError string, workspace string) (*PlanResult, error)
   138→    SubmitReducerJob(ctx context.Context, planID string, results []BranchResult, winner *BranchResult, workspace string) (*NextAction, error)
   139→}
   140→
   141→// Fanout orchestration (needs production implementation)  
   142→type FanoutOrchestrator interface {
   143→    RunHealingFanout(ctx context.Context, runCtx interface{}, branches []BranchSpec, maxParallel int) (BranchResult, []BranchResult, error)
   144→}
   145→```
   146→
   147→#### Data Structures and Configuration
   148→
   149→```go
   150→// Primary configuration structure
   151→type TransflowConfig struct {
   152→    Version      string          `yaml:"version"`
   153→    ID           string          `yaml:"id"`
   154→    TargetRepo   string          `yaml:"target_repo"`
   155→    TargetBranch string          `yaml:"target_branch"`
   156→    BaseRef      string          `yaml:"base_ref"`
   157→    Lane         string          `yaml:"lane"`
   158→    BuildTimeout string          `yaml:"build_timeout"`
   159→    Steps        []TransflowStep `yaml:"steps"`
   160→    SelfHeal     *SelfHealConfig `yaml:"self_heal"`
   161→}
   162→
   163→// Workflow execution result
   164→type TransflowResult struct {
   165→    Success        bool
   166→    WorkflowID     string
   167→    BranchName     string
   168→    CommitSHA      string
   169→    BuildVersion   string
   170→    StepResults    []StepResult
   171→    ErrorMessage   string
   172→    Duration       time.Duration
   173→    HealingSummary *TransflowHealingSummary
   174→    MRURL          string
   175→}
   176→
   177→// Job submission specifications
   178→type JobSpec struct {
   179→    Name       string                 `json:"name"`
   180→    Type       string                 `json:"type"`
   181→    HCLPath    string                 `json:"hcl_path"`
   182→    EnvVars    map[string]string      `json:"env_vars"`
   183→    Timeout    time.Duration          `json:"timeout"`
   184→    Inputs     map[string]interface{} `json:"inputs"`
   185→}
   186→```
   187→
   188→#### API Integration Points
   189→
   190→```go
   191→// Build validation endpoint integration
   192→POST /v1/apps/:app/builds?sha=...&env=dev[&lane=...]
   193→// Uses SharedPush with DeployConfig{IsPlatform: false, Environment: "dev"}
   194→
   195→// GitLab MR creation endpoints
   196→GET /api/v4/projects/{project}/merge_requests?source_branch={branch}&state=opened
   197→POST /api/v4/projects/{project}/merge_requests
   198→PUT /api/v4/projects/{project}/merge_requests/{id}
   199→```
   200→
   201→#### Environment Variables and Configuration
   202→
   203→- **GITLAB_URL**: GitLab instance URL (default: "https://gitlab.com")
   204→- **GITLAB_TOKEN**: GitLab API token (required for MR creation)
   205→- **TRANSFLOW_MODEL**: LLM model specification (default: "gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06")
   206→- **TRANSFLOW_TOOLS**: MCP tools JSON configuration
   207→- **TRANSFLOW_LIMITS**: Execution limits JSON configuration
   208→- **TRANSFLOW_SUBMIT**: Enable job submission ("1" to enable)
   209→- **TRANSFLOW_PLAN_URL/TRANSFLOW_PLAN_PATH**: Plan artifact location
   210→- **TRANSFLOW_DIFF_URL/TRANSFLOW_DIFF_PATH**: Diff artifact location
   211→- **NOMAD_ADDR**: Nomad cluster address for job submission
   212→
   213→#### File Locations and Implementation Requirements
   214→
   215→**Primary implementation files:**
   216→- CLI entry: `/internal/cli/transflow/run.go` - Command parsing and workflow coordination
   217→- Core engine: `/internal/cli/transflow/runner.go` - Workflow execution logic
   218→- Job submission: `/internal/cli/transflow/job_submission.go` - **NEEDS PRODUCTION IMPLEMENTATION**
   219→- Fanout orchestrator: `/internal/cli/transflow/fanout_orchestrator.go` - **NEEDS PRODUCTION IMPLEMENTATION**
   220→- Schema validation: `/internal/cli/transflow/schema.go` - **NEEDS VALIDATOR IMPLEMENTATION**
   221→
   222→**Supporting infrastructure:**
   223→- Configuration: `/internal/cli/transflow/config.go` - YAML parsing and validation
   224→- Integrations: `/internal/cli/transflow/integrations.go` - Dependency injection factory
   225→- Self-healing: `/internal/cli/transflow/self_healing.go` - Healing workflow types
   226→- Git provider: `/internal/git/provider/gitlab.go` - MR creation (recently implemented)
   227→- Orchestration: `/internal/orchestration/submit.go` - Nomad job submission
   228→
   229→**Job templates:**
   230→- `/roadmap/transflow/jobs/planner.hcl` - Planner job template
   231→- `/roadmap/transflow/jobs/reducer.hcl` - Reducer job template  
   232→- `/roadmap/transflow/jobs/llm_exec.hcl` - LLM execution job template
   233→- `/roadmap/transflow/jobs/orw_apply.hcl` - OpenRewrite application job template
   234→
   235→**Test files requiring completion:**
   236→- `/internal/cli/transflow/runner_test.go` - Core workflow tests
   237→- `/internal/cli/transflow/gitlab_integration_test.go` - GitLab integration tests
   238→- Integration test suite for complete end-to-end workflow validation
   239→
   240→**Configuration and documentation:**
   241→- `/roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` - Configuration template and examples
   242→- `/roadmap/transflow/MVP.md` - MVP status tracking (needs ✅ update)
   243→- `/CHANGELOG.md` - Requires update upon completion
   244→
   245→## Work Log
   246→- [2025-09-05] Created task following MVP requirements and roadmap documentation

content.content:
1→## MVP: transflow (Minimal Viable Implementation)
     2→
     3→Purpose: deliver a basic, working transflow that can apply OpenRewrite recipes, heal build errors via LangGraph planner/reducer jobs with parallel options, and open a GitLab MR.
     4→
     5→## Implementation Status
     6→
     7→✅ **Fully Implemented:**
     8→- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
     9→- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    10→- YAML configuration parsing and validation
    11→- Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)
    12→- Diff validation and application utilities
    13→- GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration
    14→- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`) with full end-to-end workflow
    15→- Test mode infrastructure with mock implementations for CI/testing
    16→
    17→⚠️ **Partially Implemented:**
    18→- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer infrastructure exists, orchestration in progress)
    19→- Self-healing config parsing and data structures
    20→- Job templates for healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
    21→
    22→❌ **Not Implemented:**
    23→- Model registry in `ployman` CLI
    24→- KB (knowledge base) read/write for learning
    25→
    26→### In-Scope (Must Work)
    27→
    28→- OpenRewrite recipe execution
    29→  - Reuse existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline (services/openrewrite-jvm, API/CLI `ploy arf transform`).
    30→  - Input: repo URL + branch, ordered recipe IDs. Output: local patch/commit applied to a workflow branch.
    31→
    32→ - LLM plan and exec
    33→  - `llm-exec` (Stream 2/Phase 1): run model with prompts + prefetched context (repo paths, HTTPS URLs), apply generated patch, commit; execute as Nomad jobs via internal/orchestration.
    34→  - `llm-plan` (Stream 2/Phase 2): produce a list of `llm-exec` steps (sequential only in MVP) from repo state + last error.
    35→  - Model registry: minimal CRUD in `ployman` CLI with schema validation; stored under `llms` namespace.
    36→  - MCP tools: declare per-step (MCP spec), env-only config; prefetch context per execution (no cache/persist).
    37→
    38→- Build check (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    39→  - Use existing build API to verify the code builds without deploying.
    40→  - Client: `internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush` (POST tarball to controller `POST /v1/apps/:app/builds?sha=...&env=dev[&lane=...]`).
    41→  - Server handler: `internal/build/trigger.go` performs lane detection (if lane not provided), builds artifacts (Unikraft/OSv/OCI/etc.), signs, generates SBOM, uploads to storage; it does not deploy to Nomad.
    42→  - Success criteria: HTTP 200; response includes `lane`, `image` or `dockerImage`. For logs, optional `GET /v1/apps/:app/logs`.
    43→  - Status endpoint (optional): `GET /v1/apps/:app/status` for additional visibility.
    44→  - App naming: derive unique app name `tfw-<transflow-id>-<timestamp>` unless explicitly provided.
    45→  - Lane override: allow explicit `lane` in transflow; fallback to auto-detect when absent.
    46→  - Timeout: request timeout configurable per run; default 10m for build check.
    47→
    48→ - Git operations + MR (Stream 3)
    49→  - Create a workflow branch at start; commit changes from steps; push branch to origin using provided token envs.
    50→  - GitLab MR: infer project from `target_repo`, target branch is `base_ref`; envs `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN`.

content.text:
# Code Review: Complete CLI Integration for Transflow MVP

## Summary
The implementation successfully addresses the major gaps in the CLI integration with comprehensive end-to-end workflow functionality. The code creates a solid test foundation using a real GitLab Java 11 Maven repository and implements proper mock infrastructure for testing. Overall, the implementation works correctly and is secure, with only minor race conditions in the test mocks that need addressing.

## 🔴 Critical Issues (0)
None found.

## 🟡 Warnings (2)

### 1. Race Condition in Mock Job Submitter
**Files**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/transflow/job_submission_test.go:30-31`
**Issue**: `MockJobSubmitter` has data races when accessed concurrently by multiple goroutines in fanout tests
**Impact**: Tests fail with race detector warnings, indicating potential test instability
**Solution**: Add mutex protection to the mock's shared state

**Code Location**:
```go
// Line 30-31 in job_submission_test.go - needs synchronization
m.CallCount++
m.Calls = append(m.Calls, call)
```

**Existing Pattern**: Other mocks in the codebase (like `MockBuildChecker`) use individual fields rather than shared slices, avoiding this pattern entirely.

### 2. Test Repository URL Hardcoded
**Files**: Multiple test files using `https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git`
**Issue**: Tests depend on external GitLab repository availability
**Impact**: Tests could fail if repository becomes unavailable or private
**Note**: This appears intentional for integration testing, but consider having a fallback mock mode for CI environments where external network access is restricted.

## 🟢 Notes (4)

### 1. Excellent Integration Test Structure
**Files**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/transflow/integration_test.go`
**Note**: The integration test architecture cleanly separates production and test modes through the `TransflowIntegrations` factory pattern. This follows the project's established dependency injection patterns well.

### 2. Comprehensive Self-Healing Implementation  
**Files**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/internal/cli/transflow/self_healing_test.go`
**Note**: The self-healing workflow tests cover both success and failure scenarios comprehensively, including retry limits and healing attempt tracking. The `HealingMockBuildChecker` pattern enables realistic failure/recovery simulation.

### 3. GitLab Provider Security Approach
**Files**: `/Users/vk/@iw2rmb/ploy/internal/git/provider/gitlab.go`
**Note**: Proper security practices implemented:
- HTTPS-only repository URLs (line 227)
- Bearer token authentication (line 94, 145, 195)
- URL encoding for API parameters (lines 85, 137, 187)
- Proper input validation for project extraction (lines 244-247)

### 4. Real Repository Creation Strategy
**Files**: Multiple test files, MVP.md documentation
**Note**: Creating `ploy-orw-java11-maven` as a real test repository provides authentic Java Maven project structure for OpenRewrite recipe testing. This enables more realistic integration testing compared to mock repositories, though it introduces external dependency.

## Recommendations

**For Production Deployment:**
1. Fix the mock race condition before merging to prevent test flakiness
2. Consider adding fallback test mode when external repository is unavailable
3. Document the test repository dependency in CI/testing documentation

**Code Quality:**
- The implementation follows established project patterns well
- Mock infrastructure is comprehensive and follows project conventions
- Integration with existing ARF pipeline is clean and reuses existing functionality appropriately
- Error handling is consistent with project patterns

**Security Posture:**
- GitLab integration properly validates inputs and uses secure authentication
- Repository URL validation prevents malicious injection attacks  
- All external API calls use proper timeout and context handling

The implementation successfully completes the CLI integration requirements and provides a solid foundation for the transflow MVP functionality.

1→# Transflow Roadmap (MVP → Iteration)
     2→
     3→Goal: Orchestrate automated code transformation flows reusing the platform’s existing building blocks (ARF, orchestration, unified storage/config), delivered in thin, incremental phases that can ship and run ASAP.
     4→
     5→Streams are designed to be largely independent, minimizing interference and enabling parallel delivery. Each stream is split into phases under `roadmap/transflow/stream-*/phase-*.md` with clear acceptance criteria and TDD guidance.
     6→
     7→## Streams Overview
     8→
     9→- Stream 1: MVP Transflow (Implemented + In Progress)
    10→  - Phase 1 ✅ (Implemented): OpenRewrite recipe → Build check (no deploy) via `/v1/apps/:app/builds`
    11→  - Phase 2 ⚠️ (In Progress): LangGraph planner/reducer as jobs for build‑error healing with parallel options (human, LLM‑exec, ORW‑generated)
    12→
    13→- Stream 2: LLM Execution + Planning
    14→  - Phase 1 ⚠️ (Partial): LLM‑Exec (sequential) as Nomad jobs, MCP context, model registry under `llms` (used by Stream 1 Phase 2 branches)
    15→  - Phase 2: LLM‑Plan (LangGraph) to emit exec steps; optional beyond MVP since planner/reducer exist in Stream 1
    16→
    17→- Stream 3: Merge Request to GitLab
    18→  - Phase 1: Create/update MR on GitLab (project from `target_repo`, target `base_ref`)
    19→  - Phase 2: Provider abstraction + GitHub (optional)
    20→
    21→## Principles
    22→
    23→- Reuse, don’t fork: Prefer `internal/arf`, `internal/orchestration`, unified storage/config, existing error envelope.
    24→- TDD + phased rollout: Keep phases small with passing unit tests; validate E2E on VPS per CLAUDE.md.
    25→- Stable interfaces: New capability should be surfaced behind existing facades where possible.
    26→
    27→## MVP Additions: LangGraph as Jobs
    28→- Planner job: Inputs = repo metadata + last_error + budgets + allowlists + KB snapshot. Output = `plan.json` with parallel options (human‑step, llm‑exec, ORW‑generated).
    29→- Orchestrator fan‑out: Submit each option as its own Nomad job (separate branch); first success wins; cancel others.
    30→- Reducer job: Inputs = run history (branch results, winner). Output = next actions (often stop; else new plan).
    31→- Learning: Jobs read/write KB in SeaweedFS (cases, summaries) and Consul KV (locks). Error signatures and patch fingerprints dedup fixes.
    32→  - See `roadmap/transflow/kb.md` for KB layout, canonicalization, compaction, and promotion rules.
    33→
    34→## Job Types (Healing Options)
    35→- human-step: Pause and wait for human intervention (e.g., push MR or commit); runner polls for branch updates.
    36→- llm-exec: Direct LLM patch generation (diff-only) with MCP context/tools; apply and build-check.
    37→- orw-gen → openrewrite: Use LLM to generate an OpenRewrite recipe (class/coords) from error context, then run the OpenRewrite job to apply and build-check.
    38→
    39→Each job type is executed as an isolated Nomad job with explicit allowlists and budgets; LangGraph plans which to launch in parallel and the reducer reconciles results.
    40→
    41→## Conventions
    42→- Branch names: `workflow/<id>/<timestamp>` (workflow) and `exec/<id>/<seq>` (future parallel).
    43→- Build app name: `tfw-<id>-<timestamp>`.
    44→- Global controls: `lane` (override), `build_timeout` (default 10m) in transflow.yaml.
    45→- MR defaults: add labels/scope `ploy`, `tfl` when supported; always include them in description.
    46→
